summary: Gruber shares his experience with Mac OS X 10.4.0 (Tiger), which he considers the buggiest release since the 10.1.x days. He highlights a specific bug encountered while logging out, where a dialog box appeared after all applications had quit — presumably asking if it was OK to quit when nothing was running. He finds philosophical humor in the absurdity of the empty confirmation dialog.
key_points: Mac OS X 10.4.0 was notably buggy, the worst since the 10.1.x era
Logout process was particularly problematic, triggering nonsensical dialog boxes
An empty or absurd confirmation dialog appeared after all apps had already quit
money_quotes: In my experience, 10.4.0 has been the buggiest release of Mac OS X since the 10.1.x days.
Admittedly, there is a sort of zen perfection to this dialog.
Perhaps it wasn't a bug at all, but rather, a life lesson.
key_insight: Even Apple's software quality stumbles sometimes, and the most absurd bugs can take on an unintentionally philosophical quality.
